content:
2026-01-31. Thinking out loud: I want a crisp intuition note for myself about PPO clipping as a 'poor man's trust region' so I can explain it to the team and decide the experiments. Start from the objective: L_CLIP(θ) = E[min(r(θ)A, clip(r(θ), 1-ε, 1+ε)A)] with r = π_θ(a|s)/π_old(a|s). Schulman et al., 2017 used ε=0.2 commonly. Intuition: clipping stops the loss from improving when r moves past 1+ε for positive advantages (and below 1-ε for negative A). That zeroes the gradient for further increase in r beyond the clip — functionally capping policy change in the direction that would increase expected return for that batch.

Why 'poor man's trust region': it enforces a per-sample hard cap on how much the ratio can help the surrogate objective, but it is not a KL constraint. It doesn't guarantee a bound on average or worst-case KL divergence; tails and distributional effects can still produce large KL even when most ratios are clipped. TRPO's constraint was E[KL] <= δ (often δ=0.01 or 0.03) and used a conjugate gradient/nat grad step to enforce that. PPO's clip is cheap, easy, and empirically works: it acts like a local projection in probability ratio space rather than a metric-aware projection in policy parameter space.

Concrete numeric example to keep in notes: A=2, r=1.5, ε=0.2 -> clip at 1.2, so objective uses 1.2*2=2.4 instead of 1.5*2=3.0; gradient for increasing r above 1.2 is zero. So clipping caps per-sample contribution to policy improvement. But if many samples have r inside [0.8,1.2], the overall step can still be large in parameter space and KL can blow up in aggregate.

Failure modes to list: advantage scale/variance too high (normalize advantages), very small batch / many epochs so overfitting to the batch, non-stationary data causing old policy mismatch, excessively large learning rate (lr), inappropriate ε: too big (0.3+) makes it weak, too small (0.05) makes it conservative and slow.

Practical heuristics I want to commit to: normalize advantages per-batch, use ε=0.2 default, monitor mean and max per-update KL (target mean KL ~0.01), implement early stopping inside an update if mean KL > 0.03, log both mean and max KL and the fraction of ratios clipped each epoch. Typical training setup I use: batch_size (timesteps)=2048, minibatch=64, epochs=10, lr=3e-4. Keep these as my baseline numbers.

Decisions I need to make now for the experiment plan: do I treat clip as the primary constraint and add KL-based early stopping as a backup? Or do I switch to a KL-penalty variant or true TRPO if stability issues appear? Also I want a measurable acceptance criterion for choosing final approach.
Do you want clip as primary with KL-based early stopping, or switch primary to explicit KL constraint from the start?

I'm leaning toward clip primary + KL early-stop as backup. Reason: implementation cost and wall-clock matters — clip is simple and parallel-friendly; TRPO or nat-grad is heavier. Practical plan: run ablation with ε in {0.1, 0.2, 0.3} and lr in {1e-4, 3e-4, 1e-3} to see sensitivity. Keep batch 2048, minibatch 64, epochs 10, seeds=5. Measure per-experiment:
- mean episodic return over time
- AUC of return up to 1M timesteps (AUC@1M)
- sample efficiency: time to reach 90% of final best return
- stability: std across seeds
- mean KL and max KL per-update, and fraction of clipped ratios per epoch

Concrete thresholds and actions: default ε=0.2; target mean KL ~0.01; early stop epochs if mean KL > 0.03 (i.e., stop current epoch and move to next batch); if AUC improves <5% over baseline and std is higher by >10% after 1M timesteps, try KL-penalty variant with β sweep {0.1, 1, 10} or fall back to TRPO. Timeline: start runs 2026-02-01, finish first pass by 2026-02-14. Branch name: ppo/clip-trust-region-jan31. Log with WandB project 'ppo-trustregion-jan26'. Save checkpoints every 200k timesteps and keep best by AUC.

Also add a small diagnostic: compute sample estimate of mean KL per minibatch as mean(old_logprob - new_logprob) and track max across minibatches; log fraction of ratios r outside [1-ε,1+ε]. Finally, decide acceptance: prefer PPO-clip config if AUC@1M improves by >=5% over base and std across seeds drops by >=10%; otherwise escalate to KL-penalty or TRPO.
Which environment and primary metric do you want to prioritize for these decisions?

Primary environment is internal sim 'ParTrans-v0' (our Paris transit/logistics sim). Prioritize AUC@1M and mean episodic return; secondary: wall-clock time per 1M timesteps and variance across seeds. Concrete logging: mean_return(t) per 1000 timesteps, AUC computed at 100k intervals, mean KL per epoch, max KL per epoch, fraction_clipped per epoch. Seeds fixed: [11,13,17,23,29]. GPUs: plan to run 8 runs in parallel across 2 nodes (4 GPUs each) starting 2026-02-01 morning CET.

Also jot down the conceptual blurb I'll share with the team: 'PPO clip is a computationally cheap, per-sample cap on the advantage-weighted probability ratio — it constrains how much each sample can push the policy but does not measure or directly bound parameter-space distance (KL). Treat clip as a heuristic projection in ratio-space; complement it with KL diagnostics and early stopping (target mean KL ≈0.01) to get closer to a true trust-region behavior without the complexity of TRPO.'

Any gaps left? I think I need a concise checklist to paste into the experiment config.
Do you want the checklist to include exact log keys, early-stop rule, hyperparam grid, and acceptance criteria?

Yes. I'll spell it out here so it's copied verbatim into the repo config:
- branch: ppo/clip-trust-region-jan31
- run_start: 2026-02-01, run_end: 2026-02-14
- env: ParTrans-v0
- seeds: [11,13,17,23,29]
- gpus: 8 (2 nodes x 4 GPUs)
- baseline hparams: batch_timesteps=2048, minibatch=64, epochs=10, lr=3e-4, ε=0.2, advantage_norm=True
- ablation grid: ε ∈ {0.1,0.2,0.3}, lr ∈ {1e-4,3e-4,1e-3}
- diagnostics/logs: mean_return@1k, AUC@100k, mean_KL@epoch, max_KL@epoch, frac_clipped@epoch, wallclock@1M
- KL estimate: mean_KL_batch = mean(old_logprob - new_logprob) per minibatch
- early-stop rule (within-epoch): if mean_KL_epoch > 0.03 stop further epochs on this batch
- checkpoint: save every 200k timesteps, retain best by AUC@1M
- acceptance criteria: choose PPO-clip if AUC@1M >= baseline_AUC*1.05 and std_return <= baseline_std*0.9; otherwise test KL-penalty β ∈ {0.1,1,10} then TRPO fallback
- repo notes: commit code and config to branch, tag run in WandB project 'ppo-trustregion-jan26'

I'll copy that into the config now. One more thought: should I also log the distribution (histogram) of r per epoch to see tail behavior?
Yes—log r histogram (e.g., bins covering [0,0.5,0.8,1.0,1.2,1.5,2.0+]) to catch tails. Ready to push the branch and schedule runs on 2026-02-01?
